gates.description: Full internal RL should beat matched controls on held-out terminal success and strong sparse-reward success, or win tied sparse-reward outcomes with stronger mechanism evidence.
Discovered abstract-action families should be reused across cases, not recreated every time.
Held-out subgoal recombinations should remain solvable with non-trivial completion.
Delayed sparse reward should align with the abstract-action windows that preceded success.
Internal RL should show concrete parameter-update evidence during training, not only better outcomes.
Stronger ETA claims require batch-level rollout evidence, non-trivial replacement effect, and bounded held-out variance.
The proof should persist across trace and synthetic-open-weight backends.
